message:
chore(beads): expand jfp save completion notes

Update bead jeffreysprompts.com-9g76 with detailed close_reason covering auth checks, premium validation, API integration, and --json support.

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-94r0","title":"Add search integration for personal prompts in CLI","description":"Add search integration for personal prompts in CLI.

When user is authenticated with premium account, extend search to include:
- User's own prompts (if they have any)
- User's saved prompts
- User's collections (search collection names and descriptions)
- User's prompt notes

Search Flow:
1. Search local prompt registry first (fast, always available)
2. If authenticated, concurrently search premium API endpoints
3. Merge results, marking personal prompts with [personal] tag
4. Display combined results with source indicators

CLI Options:
- jfp search <query> --mine (search only personal prompts)
- jfp search <query> --saved (search only saved prompts)
- jfp search <query> --all (search everything, default for premium users)
- jfp search <query> --local (search only local registry, default for free users)

Graceful Degradation:
- If API unavailable, fall back to local-only search with warning
- If not authenticated, only search local registry (no warning)

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9g76","title":"Implement jfp save <prompt-id> command","description":"Implement jfp save command to save prompts to user's premium account.

Usage: jfp save <prompt-id>

Features:
- Requires premium authentication (prompts user to login if not authenticated)
- Validates prompt ID exists in local registry before saving
- POST to /api/cli/saved-prompts with { prompt_id }
- Displays success message with saved prompt title
- Handles duplicate saves gracefully (already saved message)
- Requires premium tier (show upgrade message for free users)

Error Handling:
- 401: Prompt to login
- 403: Display "Saving prompts requires premium subscription"
- 404: Display "Prompt not found"
- Network errors: Display connection error message

Output Format:
- Default: "Saved: [prompt title]"
- With --json: { saved: true, prompt_id, title, saved_at }

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9ho","title":"Initialize monorepo structure with Bun workspaces -- Background: This project uses a monorepo architecture with packages/core (shared prompts/search/exports), apps/web (Next.js 16), and CLI (jfp.ts). Using Bun workspaces enables dependency sharing between packages. The monorepo structure follows brennerbot.org patterns for consistency. This is foundational - all other work depends on it.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:26:50.541605041-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:16:55.442579082-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:16:55.442579082-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented monorepo structure, core package with types, registry, search engine (BM25, tokenizer, synonyms), and export functions. CLI is working."}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","title":"Add --json flag support for all new CLI commands","description":"Add --json flag support to all new CLI commands for machine-readable output.

Commands to Update:
- jfp login (output: { authenticated: true, email, tier })
- jfp logout (output: { logged_out: true })
- jfp whoami (output: { email, tier, user_id })
- jfp collections list (output: { collections: [...] })
- jfp collections show (output: { collection, items: [...] })
- jfp notes (output: { prompt_id, notes: [...] })
- jfp save (output: { saved: true, prompt_id })
- jfp list --mine/--saved (output: { prompts: [...] })

Implementation:
- Add --json/-j flag to all relevant commands
- When --json is set, output only valid JSON to stdout
- Errors should also be JSON: { error: true, message, code }
- Progress/status messages go to stderr when --json is enabled
- Ensure consistent field naming across all commands
